questions.question: Does "tests for CRUD" mean create/read only for comments, or are update/delete operations also expected even though the requested API surface only names GET/POST /v1/markets/{id}/comments?
questions.why_it_matters: The current router is fully explicit and there is no generic resource layer, so supporting update/delete would expand the backend routes, client API, UI controls, and test matrix beyond the two endpoints in the brief.

questions.question: What should the POST comment request body contain, and what identity should a comment be attributed to: accountId, a separate author/display name, or anonymous posting?
questions.why_it_matters: The frontend only has session.accountId plus an optional agentId header today, and the backend has no user/profile model to derive comment authorship from.

questions.question: Should POST /v1/markets/{id}/comments inherit the existing protected-write behavior around X-Bayes-Agent-Id validation and per-agent rate limiting when auth is enabled?
questions.why_it_matters: All existing market-scoped POST routes are categorized by resolve_write_request_agent(...); comments either need to plug into that policy system or intentionally bypass it.

questions.question: What is the canonical comment resource shape in API responses: commentId, seq, marketId, author/accountId, body/content, createdAt, updatedAt, and any replay/idempotency metadata?
questions.why_it_matters: There is no existing comment type to extend, so the chosen shape drives backend storage, frontend types, test fixtures, and whether comments can support pagination and future edits cleanly.

questions.question: What pagination contract should GET /v1/markets/{id}/comments use, and in what order should comments be returned: oldest-first, newest-first, or a cursor anchored on a monotonic sequence?
questions.why_it_matters: The only paginated collection in the repo today is /v1/markets/{id}/events, which uses fromSeq/limit/nextFromSeq; comments can mirror that pattern or establish a new one.

questions.question: What validation and rendering constraints apply to comment bodies: maximum length, blank trimming, duplicate submission handling, and whether text is plain text only or supports formatting such as Markdown?
questions.why_it_matters: This determines server-side validation, frontend form behavior, error cases, and whether the UI can safely render comments without introducing formatting or sanitization ambiguity.

questions.question: Should comments be allowed on all market statuses, or should posting be limited to active markets while resolved/closed/draft markets remain read-only?
questions.why_it_matters: The market detail page already shows and hides panels based on market status, so composer visibility and POST validation need a consistent product rule.

questions.question: Should comment writes also appear in the existing market event journal, or should comments live in a separate per-market discussion store with its own sequencing and pagination?
questions.why_it_matters: The backend already maintains a canonical per-market event chain, but the feature brief describes a discussion thread; choosing one model affects storage reuse, tests, and whether comments become part of the journaled market history.

questions.question: Is in-memory persistence acceptable for comments, matching the rest of this backend, or is durable storage expected across process restarts?
questions.why_it_matters: The current service stores markets, orders, events, and rate-limit state in process-local dictionaries and resets them heavily in tests; durable comments would be a materially different implementation.

questions.question: On the market detail page, should the comment composer follow the existing session pattern and only enable posting when an accountId is configured, or should the UI expose anonymous/read-only states differently for discussion?
questions.why_it_matters: Current write panels either hide entirely or rely on the shared session context, so comments need a deliberate UX choice instead of inheriting that behavior accidentally.

assumptions.assumption: Comments can be stored in the same in-memory style as MARKETS and EVENTS, and should reset with server.reset_state() in tests.
assumptions.if_wrong: A durable store or different lifecycle would require new persistence plumbing and a different testing strategy.

assumptions.assumption: This feature only requires create/read behavior for comments despite the brief using the word CRUD.
assumptions.if_wrong: We would need additional update/delete endpoints, UI affordances, and broader API/client coverage.

assumptions.assumption: Comment identity will be based on the existing session accountId, with the optional agentId header only used for backend write controls rather than display.
assumptions.if_wrong: We would need a separate author model or additional request fields and frontend inputs.

assumptions.assumption: GET /v1/markets/{id}/comments should reuse the repo's existing pagination style, most likely limit plus a forward-moving sequence cursor similar to /events.
assumptions.if_wrong: A different ordering or cursor scheme would change both the contract and the frontend data-fetching logic.

assumptions.assumption: Comments should be rendered as a dedicated discussion section on the market detail page and kept separate from the existing event journal.
assumptions.if_wrong: If comments must be folded into the journal, the feature becomes more of an event-stream extension than a standalone thread UI.

assumptions.assumption: The composer should follow the current session UX and allow posting only when an accountId is configured, while leaving the thread readable without session state.
assumptions.if_wrong: We would need a different unauthenticated UX, such as anonymous posting or a stronger requirement for agent/account setup before the section appears.
